hook.text: Julius Caesar encrypted his military messages by shifting every letter three positions.

buildup.text: A becomes D, B becomes E, C becomes F. The entire alphabet slides forward.

discovery.text: This is modular arithmetic in disguise: each letter shifts by 3, mod 26.

twist.text: With only 25 possible shifts, anyone can crack it by trying all options in minutes.

climax.text: Caesar's cipher was trivial, but it planted the seed for all modern encryption.

punchline.text: The weakest cipher launched the strongest idea.

quiz.question: How does a Caesar cipher encrypt a message?
options: By shifting each letter a fixed number of positions
